instructions.instruction: Dependencies must point inward only — inner layers (entities, use cases) must never import from outer layers (adapters, frameworks)
instructions.original_snippets: Dependency Rule: Dependencies point inward only. Inner layers know nothing about outer layers.
instructions.relevant_when: reviewing or designing module import structure

instructions.instruction: Entities must be pure business objects — no ORM decorators, framework types, or infrastructure imports allowed in the entity layer
instructions.original_snippets: Entities remain pure (no framework or infrastructure imports). BAD: Entity imports ORM decorators, framework types. GOOD: Entities are plain objects; adapters handle framework mapping.

instructions.instruction: Use cases must define explicit input/output ports as interfaces and depend on repository abstractions, never on concrete implementations
instructions.original_snippets: Use cases: Have one responsibility, depend on abstractions (ports), not concrete implementations. BAD: Use case instantiates concrete PostgresRepository. GOOD: Use case depends on IRepository interface; DI provides implementation.
instructions.relevant_when: designing use case classes or orchestration logic

instructions.instruction: Controllers must be thin — they translate HTTP input to use case input and use case output to HTTP response; no business logic in controllers
instructions.original_snippets: BAD: Controller validates, calculates, and persists data. GOOD: Controller calls use case; use case orchestrates business logic.
instructions.relevant_when: writing HTTP controllers or route handlers

instructions.instruction: Circular dependencies between modules are forbidden — extract a shared contract/interface and invert the dependency
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER allow circular dependencies. BAD: Module A imports B and B imports A. GOOD: Extract shared contract/module and invert dependencies.
instructions.relevant_when: resolving circular import errors or reviewing module structure

instructions.instruction: Framework and infrastructure code (ORM, Express, DI containers) must live in the outermost layer — the domain must remain framework-free
instructions.original_snippets: Keep frameworks (Express, NestJS, TypeORM, React) in the infrastructure/adapter layer. Domain remains framework-free.
instructions.relevant_when: placing framework configuration or integration code

instructions.instruction: NEVER design architectural boundaries for imagined future requirements — only introduce a boundary when the current need justifies the cost
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER design boundaries for imagined future requirements. BAD: Add full hexagonal architecture 'in case' of future DB migration. GOOD: Solve current need; refactor when trigger appears (YAGNI).
instructions.relevant_when: deciding whether to introduce a new architectural boundary

instructions.instruction: Document significant boundary decisions in an ADR with rationale, alternatives, chosen option, and risks
instructions.original_snippets: Document Boundary Decisions — ADR with rationale, alternatives, and risks.
instructions.relevant_when: introducing a new service boundary or major architectural change
